acts.summary: Sets out the powers of the Minister of Transportation and Transit over provincial highways and highway right-of-way, including contracting for transportation projects, dealing with land (compensation, expropriation, disposition), and continuing the BC Transportation Financing Authority. Assented to May 13, 2004; verified current to November 26, 2024 on the official BC Laws consolidation.

sections.title: Minister's rights, powers and advantages over highway right-of-way
sections.description: Gives the minister rights, powers and advantages over provincial highway land, including entry, expropriation, injurious affection and disposition of land, which underpin the ministry's highway use permit system for access, works, events and rural subdivision referrals.

services.name: Apply for a highway access permit for commercial or development access
services.ms_name: Get a permit to build, access or use a provincial highway

services.name: Apply for a highway access permit for a rural home or farm driveway
services.ms_name: Get a permit to build, access or use a provincial highway

services.name: Apply for a highway works permit to build or repair near a highway
services.ms_name: Get a permit to build, access or use a provincial highway

services.name: Apply for a highway event permit
services.ms_name: Get a permit to build, access or use a provincial highway

services.name: Get a subdivision development referral for rural land near a highway
services.ms_name: Get a permit to build, access or use a provincial highway

acts.summary: Establishes the Registrar of Passenger Transportation and the Passenger Transportation Board, and licenses taxis, limousines, inter-city buses, shuttles and ride-hail (transportation network services) operating in B.C. Assented to May 13, 2004; verified current to December 31, 2024 on the official BC Laws consolidation. Replaced the former Motor Carrier Act.

sections.title: Registrar of Passenger Transportation and licensee records
sections.description: Establishes the Registrar of Passenger Transportation, who maintains the public index of licensees, permit holders and application decisions used in the passenger transportation registry.

services.name: Find a licensed taxi or ride-hail company in your area
services.ms_name: Use a licensed taxi, limousine or ride-hail service

sections.title: Licences and temporary operating permits under Part 4
sections.description: Sets out how a person applies for, and the registrar or board issues, a licence or temporary operating permit authorizing a general passenger vehicle, inter-city bus, passenger directed vehicle or transportation network services authorization.

services.name: Apply for a passenger transportation licence to operate a taxi, limo or ride-hail business
services.ms_name: Use a licensed taxi, limousine or ride-hail service

sections.title: Vehicle identifiers and driver requirements
sections.description: Defines vehicle identifiers, passenger directed vehicles and related terms that underpin driver and vehicle requirements for taxi, limousine and ride-hail service.

services.name: Find requirements to drive a taxi, limousine or ride-hail vehicle
services.ms_name: Use a licensed taxi, limousine or ride-hail service

acts.summary: Continues British Columbia Transit as a corporation and sets out its purposes: planning, acquiring, constructing, maintaining and operating public passenger transportation systems, including transit service areas outside Metro Vancouver and services such as HandyDART. Verified current to September 3, 2024 on the official BC Laws consolidation.

sections.title: Continuation and purposes of British Columbia Transit
sections.description: Continues British Columbia Transit as a corporation and sets its purposes and objects, including planning and operating public passenger transportation systems that support regional growth and community plans.

services.name: Find BC Transit routes and schedules in your community
Get accessible door-to-door transit through HandyDART

acts.full_name: South Coast British Columbia Transportation Authority Act
acts.summary: Establishes the South Coast British Columbia Transportation Authority (TransLink) and its powers to plan, acquire, construct and operate rail transportation systems, busways and ancillary works in Metro Vancouver. Assented to July 30, 1998; verified current to October 29, 2024 on the official BC Laws consolidation.

sections.title: Establishment of the transportation authority
sections.description: Establishes the South Coast British Columbia Transportation Authority and defines its rail transportation systems, busways and ancillary works, forming the legal basis for TransLink's transit operations and major rapid transit capital projects in Metro Vancouver.

services.name: Find TransLink routes and schedules in Metro Vancouver
Dispute a TransLink fare evasion ticket
Rapid transit and major capital project delivery
